title: Verbatim-copy CI lint for source-documentation copyright compliance
context: PLAN.md v0.2 introduces a source-documentation copyright risk that is distinct from the dataset license: a dataset can be fully open while its authoritative documentation prose (WHO ICD-O-3 manual, SEER coding manuals) is copyrighted text. Verbatim lifting of that prose violates copyright independently of the dataset's open status. The v0.2 plan mandates a paraphrase-with-citation rule and a verbatim-copy lint that runs in CI, caps quoted-run length against source manuals, and requires attribution on any quoted snippet — failing the build on long unattributed runs. No existing task implements this lint (validator-006 enforces schema provenance completeness, not prose copyright). This risk is listed in the v0.2 risk table with a 'medium' probability and 'medium' consequence.
objective: Implement a verbatim-copy CI lint that checks dictionary definition prose against a configurable threshold: flags or fails the build on runs of text above a length cap that lack an inline attribution (sourceRef), preventing inadvertent copyright infringement of WHO ICD-O-3 / SEER manual prose.
acceptanceCriteria: Lint checks the 'description' and 'caveats' text fields in every dictionary entry against a configurable maximum run length for quoted/verbatim text without an explicit sourceRef attribution.

A run of prose that exceeds the length cap without an inline attribution citation causes the lint to fail with a human-readable message identifying the field, the offending passage, and the required action (paraphrase or add attribution).
Short attributed snippets (below the cap, or carrying an explicit sourceRef on the enclosing allowedValues or field entry) pass the lint.
The length cap and attribution-marker convention are documented in CONTRIBUTING.md or equivalent and configurable via a project-level config file.
Golden fixtures in CI demonstrate: (a) a clean dictionary passes; (b) a dictionary with a long unattributed prose run fails with the correct diagnostic.
Lint is wired into the existing CI validation pipeline so it runs alongside the validator-006 provenance check; separate from the schema/provenance checks.
Code MIT; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint green; DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md (v0.2) — §Solution pipeline 3 (paraphrase-with-citation rule), §Compliance (source-documentation copyright subsection), §Risk table (verbatim copying row)
tasks/cancer-data-dictionaries-validator-006.json (existing CI validation pipeline to integrate into)
tasks/cancer-data-dictionaries-model-001.json (canonical model field structure: description, caveats, sourceRef)
output: PR to the repo adding the verbatim-copy lint tool, CI wiring, configuration schema, golden fixtures, and CONTRIBUTING.md documentation of the paraphrase-with-citation rule.
